definition: Use secure locality-sensitive indexes, encrypted distance comparison, homomorphic or secure multiparty protocols, or federated hashing to find near-neighbor pairs without centralizing raw sensitive data. This is relevant when SPECTRA needs cross-site train-eval similarity under privacy constraints.

scale_profile.best_for: Clinical, genomic, and institutional audits where train and eval records live across parties that cannot share raw data.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Large multi-party datasets when candidate generation is sublinear and cryptographic verification is restricted to candidates.
scale_profile.avoid_when: All data can be legally and scientifically centralized; ordinary exact or ANN search is simpler and easier to inspect.

tunable_parameters.name: privacy_protocol
tunable_parameters.purpose: Selects secure LSH, encrypted distance comparison, homomorphic encryption, or secure multiparty computation.

tunable_parameters.name: sketch_or_bucket_size
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls candidate recall and privacy leakage tradeoffs.

tunable_parameters.name: verification_budget
tunable_parameters.purpose: Limits expensive secure pair verification to likely candidates.

quality_gates: Document the threat model and what information is revealed by sketches, buckets, scores, or pair outputs.
Estimate recall against a non-private or locally exact subset when possible.
Check that site identity, missingness, or batch structure does not become a hidden similarity proxy.
failure_modes: Privacy constraints can hide enough detail to make the SPECTRA axis hard to validate.
Hash bucket collisions or encryption-compatible approximations can bias similarity estimates.
Pairwise outputs themselves may leak sensitive membership or relatedness information.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py only as a local non-private reference; a privacy-preserving implementation should emit the same pairwise CSV after secure candidate generation or verification.

citations.title: A framework for privacy-preserving similarity search of massive multi-party genomic data
citations.evidence: Combines privacy-enhanced LSH with federated genomic analysis to find similar records without sharing raw data.

citations.title: Sub-Linear Privacy-Preserving Near-Neighbor Search
citations.evidence: Introduces secure locality-sensitive indexing for sublinear private near-neighbor search.

citations.title: Secure discovery of genetic relatives across large-scale and distributed genomic data sets
citations.evidence: Uses federated hashing and secure protocols to identify related genomic records across distributed datasets.
